page_Text:
Purpose of HolyLandPhotos.org

The purpose of HolyLandPhotos.org is to make images from the lands of the Bible available free to the general public. Our emphasis is upon the images. The text accompanying the image is meant to be helpful in identifying what one is looking at in the image and to comment briefly about the significance of the image, but the text is not intended to be exhaustive. The interested person should pursue avenues of interest in relevant dictionaries and encyclopedias.

The site has been conceived, developed, and maintained on a volunteer basis by Dr. Carl Rasmussen and Jesse Gavin (see below). Carl Rasmussen provides all photos unless otherwise noted.

Please note — this is a GROWING site. Images are being added to it weekly.

Use of the Web Site

Classroom  — For those with classroom connections to the internet, you can merely log on to www.HolyLandPhotos.org and click away to show images to your class.  You are invited to include references/links to the web site in your syllabai and web pages that you maintain - so that your students can access the site for study purposes.

Bible Study  — HolyLandPhotos.org can be used by those studying the Bible. As you become interested in various places check HolyLandPhotos.org to see if images of the place or area that you are studying are available in the digital database. This can be done by checking the site list, using the search feature, or browsing by country and region.

Digital Presentations — The images in the HolyLandPhotos.org are not just for viewing, but are available for you to download and use in your digital presentations: in the classroom, in churches, in synagogues, in religious schools, etc. For downloading permission click here.

Current and Anticipated Coverage of HolyLandPhotos.org

The current coverage of this web site includes archaeological sites and views in Israel, Turkey, Greece, Italy, Malta, Jordan, and Lebanon. About 10–15 images are being added weekly.  You are invited to register here for occasional announcements on image additions.

The Images

The images in the database are JPEG format.  It has not been possible to be entirely consistent with image size or quality. We will be entering our best quality images, and will be upgrading images as better ones become available.  Look in the upper rightt portion of the page to click to download the best available version of an image.

Carl Rasmussen

Dr. Rasmussen is Professor Emeritis of Biblical Studies at Bethel College (MN). Prior to coming to Bethel in 1980 he was Dean of the Institute of Holy Land Studies in Jerusalem for seven years. He has been teaching and guiding academic groups in the Middle East since 1973. For the past forty years he has been guiding and teaching academic groups in Israel, Jordan, Turkey, Greece, Italy, and Malta for about two months each year. During these trips he has been constantly adding to his digital archive, and those images form the basis of this site.

He has his Ph.D. in Ancient Near Eastern Studies, and also Th.M., B.D., and B.A. degrees. He is the author of the award-winning Zondervan Atlas of the Bible and the history, archaeology, and geography editor for the new, revised, version of Halley's Bible Handbook.

Dr. Rasmussen provides both the textual and photographic content of the web site.

Jesse Gavin

Jesse Gavin is a 2001 graduate of Bethel College (MN). He has creatively developed and continues to maintain the web site on a volunteer basis! He currently works as a Senior Software Developer at Dow Jones. His creative and technical abilities have made this site into a joy to use.

Invitation to Submit Your Images

If you posses digital images of sites not included in HolyLandPhotos.org we would invite you to submit them for possible inclusion in the database. This would be a great way to increase our coverage and to make your best images available to the public.

If you have some images that you would like to submit, feel free to contact Dr. Rasmussen. There is no remuneration for this, but we would of course credit you as the provider of the image.

For a sample of an image provided by a "contributor to the site" click here.

Special thanks

To the Bethel Alumni Association for a series of grants to help defray some of the costs involved in the construction of this site.

To the Regents of Bethel College and Seminary for providing the faculty with time for sabbatical learning and growth.

To the Jerusalem University College for maintaining a fine academic institution in Jerusalem.

The images in this collection, and the text, were produced by Dr. Carl Rasmussen - Professor of Biblical Studies at Bethel College. These Web pages were designed, and are maintained, by Jesse Gavin

Permission to Use General
These images are our gift to you for non–commercial/non–web personal use—but see below for web and commercial usage). The only request that we have is that you use something like the following credit at sometime during your presentation.

"Image(s) courtesy of www.HolyLandPhotos.org" - either in written or verbal form.

Commercial Usage
To request permission to use images commercially—for a modest fee, contact Dr. Rasmussen at HolyLandPhotos@gmail.com.  I will be able to provide you with non-watermarked, high resolution images.

Web Usage
In general, the images are NOT to be used on other Web sites - however, permission is granted to use up to 4 images per web site IF clear credit is given to www.HolyLandPhotos.org for each image used.  For the use of 5 or more images, you must have the express permission of www.HolyLandPhotos.org.  Text is not to be reproduced without specific permission from content provider.

Register for Updates
To receive an occasional notification when images are added, Register Here.  Please note, you will not be swamped with a lot of Email.  Also, this list will not be used or shared with any other party!

Additional Images
To request an image(s) that you would like to see placed on this site, contact Dr. Rasmussen at HolyLandPhotos@gmail.com.

Archaeology and Geography

Hoerth, Alfred J. Archaeology and the Old Testament. Grand Rapids, MI: Baker, 1998. A great source of information regarding the archaeology of the Old Testament - arranged chronologically, and very well illustrated. Click Here to order at amazon.com.

McRay, John. Archaeology and the New Testament. Grand Rapids, MI: Baker, 1991. A great source of information regarding New Testament archaeology - including Israel, Turkey, Greece, and Italy. Click Here to order at amazon.com.

Rasmussen, Carl G. A brand new edition of the award winning The Zondervan Atlas of the Bible is now available. Grand Rapids: Zondervan, 2010. A great geographical introduction to the lands of the Bible written by the site content provider. First fifth is a geographical introduction to the area, the middle three-fifths is an historical-geographical commentary on the whole Bible, and the final fifth a set of appendices, including a Gazetteer. Click Here to order at amazon.com.

I am pleased to announce that Zondervan has released a 13 Lesson Video series that we filmed in the Holy Land.  This is an 18-minute Introduction to the ancient Middle East.  A Trailer for the series can be found Here.  This video is part of their "MasterLectures" and is available streaming there and also as a DVD set on amazon.com.  For an announcement on Todd Bolen's blog, Click Here.

Two samples of this 13 Lesson Series are available on YouTube.

  1. Here is an 18-minute Introduction to the Middle East.
  2. Here is a 6-minute video Introduction to Jerusalem.

Guide Books

Murphy-O'Connor, Jerome. The Holy Land: An Oxford Archaeological Guide from Earliest Times to 1700. Fifth edition, revised and expanded. Oxford: Oxford University Press, 2008.  Simply the best - and inexpensive!!  Click Here to order at amazon.com.

McDonagh, Bernard. Blue Guide: Turkey. Third edition, atlas, maps and plans by RJS Associates and John Flower. New York: WW Norton, 1966. One of the best historical/archaeological guides to Turkey. Click Here to order at amazon.com.

Barber, Robin and Flower, John.  Blue Guide: Greece.  Sixth edition.  New York: WW Norton, 2001. One of the best historical/archaeological guides to Greece .  Click Here to order at amazon.com.

Mee, Christopher, and Spawforth, Antony. Greece: An Oxford Archaeological Guide. Oxford Archaeological Guides, edited by Barry Cunliffe. Oxford: Oxford University Press, 2001.  Compare O'Connor above.  Click Here to order at amazon.com.

For those who wish to walk in Galilee check out The Jesus Trail and related walks.

Ancient Life, History, and Culture

Connolly, Peter, and Dodge, Hazel. The Ancient City: Life in Classical Athens & Rome. New York: Oxford University Press, 1998.  Filled with very helpful pictures and diagrams.  Excellent for general background of both Greek and Roman culture - and inexpensive!!  Click Here to order at amazon.com.

Life of Paul

Ramsay, William M. St. Paul — The Traveler and Roman Citizen. Full–Color Updated & Revised Edition, edited by Mark W. Wilson. Grand Rapids, MI: Kregel, 2001.  A classic, updated with excellent photos and supplimentary commentary - covers the whole life of Paul.  Click Here to order at amazon.com.

Quick Start
If you know the name of the place that you are looking for, check the alphabetical "Site List" OR type in the name in the "Search Window" and click search.

Registering for "UpDates" will keep you abreast of what has been added to this Site (promise: you will not be inundated with Email!).

There are several ways to find images on this Site.

Site List
Self-evident and alphabetical. If you cannot find a place, check alternate spellings because different translations of the Bible will sometimes spell names differently. For example: Chorazin (NASB, NRSV) vs. Korazin (NIV) or Beth-Shan (NASB, NRSV) vs. Beth Shan (NIV). Fortunately, such instances are not common.

Browse by Region
Images are cataloged by country, regions within a country, and then by sites within those regions.

Search
A topical "Search" feature is provided.  Typical entries that can be searched are Geographical Names, Personal Names, Archaeological Periods, various topics, artifacts, etc.  If a "search word" does not yield results, try an  alternate spelling and/or a related term.

Viewing Images
To enlarge an image - click on it.
To close the enlarged image - click on it.

To download images to your computer - follow your browser's typical method AND see "Download" in the lower left portion of your screen.

For images used, please credit as follows: "Image(s) courtesy of HolyLandPhotos.org" - either in written or verbal form OR www.HolyLandPhotos.org.

The following “sites” are related to the Last Week of Jesus’ earthly ministry and his crucifixion and resurrection.

Don’t miss what WAS the best-preserved “Rolling Stone Tomb” in Israel — until it was destroyed.

Church of the Holy Sepulcher — probable site of crucifixion and burial/resurrection of Jesus.

Gordon’s Calvary and Garden Tomb — alternative (Protestant) site of crucifixion and burial/resurrection of Jesus.

Gethsemane — site of Jesus praying and his arrest.

Mount of Olives — site of Palm Sunday entrance into Jerusalem and Jesus’ “Olivet Discourse.”

Via Dolorosa — sacred pilgrim route in Jerusalem.

The following books are for sale.  If you want to purchase any, send an email to HolyLandPhotos@gmail.com to see if they are still available.

GEOGRAPHY

Rasmussen, Carl, and Monson, James.  Student Map 'A' (Patriarchs through Ezra-Nehemiah) and Student Map 'B' (Ezra-Nehemiah through Justinian) — 1:275,000.  Two maps of Israel from Dan to Elath.  Four sheets, each sheet 32.5 x 24.5 inches.  Jerusalem: Pictorial Archive, 1975.  New.  Three sets for $10.00.  About 20 sets are available.

ARCHAEOLOGY

Finegan, Jack.  The Archeology of the New Testament: The Life of Jesus and the Beginning of the Early Church.  First Edition.  Princeton, NJ: Princeton University, 1969.  Hardcover, a few underlines.  $10.00.

AKKADIAN AND BABYLONIAN, EGYPTIAN HIEROGLYPHICS, ARABIC, and SYRIAC

Faulkner, Raymond O.  A Concise Dictionary of Middle Egyptian.  Oxford: Griffith Institute, Oxford, 1962.  Hardcover, not marked.  $25.00.

Mercer, Samuel A. B.  An Egyptian Grammar with Chrestomathy and Glossary.  New York: Ungar, 1961.  Hardcover, unmarked.  $5.00.

Aviram, J.  Studies in Egyptology and Linguistics in Honour of H. J. Polotsky.  Jerusalem: Israel Exploration Society, 1964.  Hardcover, not marked.  $20.00.

Gleason, H. A., Jr.  An Introduction to Descriptive Linguistics.  Revised edition.  New York: Holt, Rinehart and Winston, 1961.  Hardcover, some markings. $8.00.

n.a.  Psalterium Syriace.  London: Societatem Bibliophilorum, 1914.  Hardcover, unmarked.  $5.00.

BIBLICAL STUDIES

Gundry, Robert H.  A Survey of the New Testament.  Third edition.  Grand Rapids, MI: Zondervan, 1994.  Hardcover, about 10 pages with minor markings. $8.00

Hamilton, Victor P.  Handbook on The Pentateuch.  Grand Rapids, MI: Baker Books, 1982.  Hardcover, like new, unmarked.  $8.00.

I like your latest (for me) material on the HolyLandPhotos.org web site.  It, by the way, is a requirement for my class to visit it. -- College Professor on the east coast USA  3/26/05

I just want to say a special thanks for the wonderful photos you have made available on your web site. I am using them in Bible lessons here in our very small church in Krakow, Poland. -- 3/22/05

In July I am to give a public presentation on Israel in the light of the Bible.  Have looked around for picture resources and find your site to be the best in terms of the variety it offers and also the fact that the photos are already in an electronic format. -- Pastor in Tasmania — 3/22/05

Thanks for the message related to the website.  Professor XX and Professor YY are both using the website as a source for great pictures in the classes I have with them right now. -- Seminary Student in western USA 3/17/05

Over the past weekend, someone gave me the info concerning your website "Holy Land Photos", and I have just spent the last half hour perusing the site. I am a pastor in the Reformed Church in America and have been teaching about Israel and Turkey in recent years, and your collection of pictures and summaries are awesome!!! I've got a congregation that is hungry for exactly this type of thing, and we will use your site (with great sensitivity to copyright matters) as a great resource for getting people in touch with the lands of the Bible, and of Jesus.

 I just wanted to thank you for your diligence in study and archiving all this, and your generosity in sharing all of this with the church!  My guess is that this website will become a significant tool for many of our members in their study of God's Word. -- Pastor of a Reformed Church in Idaho 3/17/05

For your convenience on this page I have assembled some suggestions for images related to a variety of topics.  For example, if you are studying the life of Solomon I have assembled images that are related to that topic that you might want to check.  As always, you are welcome to use our images in your personal PowerPoint presentations—if you could give credit (and/or a link) to www.HolyLandPhotos.org that would be appreciated.

Topics: 

History of Israel;

New Testament:

Recommended Readings; Travel.

History of Israel — I taught this course for many years to University Students and approached the topic chronologically—moving from the earliest to later periods.  I have begun to assemble recommended images for each of the historical periods that I covered.  So here goes.

Early Bronze Age (EBA, 3150–2200 B.C.)     Completed 25 August 2013.

  • Arad — 19 images of EB houses, city wall, towers, temple, water system, etc.
  • Ai (et-Tel) — 3 images of EB wall, temple.
  • Megiddo — 9 images of  EB temples, altar.
  • Egypt — Pyramids

Intermediate Bronze Age (IBA, 2250–2000 B.C.; possibly the time of Abraham)     Completed 25 August 2013.

  • IBA settlements in the Negev (southern Israel; 5 images)
  • IBA shaft tombs (north of Jerusalem at Dhahr Mirzbaneh [Ein Samiya]; 8 images)
  • Dolmens (Golan Heights northeast of the Sea of Galilee; 3 images)

Middle Bronze Age II (MB II, 2000–1500 B.C.; the time of the Patriarchs of Genesis 12–50)     Completed 26 August 2013.

  • Shechem — 10 images of the massive city wall, gates, Balage Balogh diagrams
  • Gezer — images of MB II gate city wall, and standing stones/high place
  • Dan — 5 images of the wonderfully intact MB II city gate
  • Hebron — 25 images of the Cave of Machpelah
  • Jerusalem
    • Pool Tower (above the Gihon Spring; 9 images)
    • Water Channel II (near the Gihon Spring; 9 images)
  • Ashqelon — 8 images of the MB II Gate and Glacis
  • Jericho — 2 images of the MB II Revetment Wall
  • Megiddo — 1 image of MB II/LB City Gate
  • Shiloh — 4 images of the MB II City Wall

 

Solomon (ca. 970–930 B.C.)       (Under development 27 August 2013)

  • City Gates and Walls at Hazor, Megiddo, Gezer
  • Gezer Calendar
  • Fortresses in the Negev — Arad Citadel, Haluqim
  • Jerusalem — Temple Mount;  el-Aqsa Mosque (site of Solomonic Palace?)
  • Parallels to the Solomonic Temple  — Ain Dara in Syria; Cedars of Lebanon
  • Parallel to Solomonic Palace — the bit hilanni Palace at Alalakh in Turkey
  • Geshur — home town of Solomon's brother Absalom
  • Beth Shemesh — 6 images of a huge cistern

 

New Testament

Places Mentioned in the Gospels:

Nazareth (39 images in 3 folders), Bethlehem (32 images),  Bethany Beyond the Jordan (Jordanian; Israeli), Cana of Galilee, Pools of Bethesda (in Jerusalem), Capernaum, Chorazin (site; synagogue), Bethsaida, Magdala, Mount of Beatitudes, Loaves and Fish Church, Church of the Primacy of Peter,  Gergesa (Kursi), Tiberias (city; gate and theater; from above), Sychar, Mt. Gerizim, Gadara (city, theater, churches, artifacts, area), Caesarea Philippi, Mount of Transfiguration (Tabor), Jericho, Mt. of Olives (views; Dominus Flevit; Mary Magdalene Church; Gethsemane, cave of Gethsemane), Mosque of Ascension.

Paul's First Missionary Journey (Acts 13:1–14:28) 

Antioch on the Orontes, Seleucia Pieria, Paphos, Perga (city, stadium, theater, bath), Pisidian Antioch (artifacts), Iconium, Lystra, Derbe, Attalia.  (Completed 3 September 2013)

Paul's Second Missionary Journey (Acts 15:36–18:22) 

Antioch on the Orontes, Seleucia Pieria, Derbe, Lystra, Iconium, Troas, Samothrace, Neapolis, Phlippi (Lydia Baptismal [Thyatira]; South Philippi, North Philippi), Amphipolis (more to come), Thessaloniki (Forum, City, St. Demetrius Church, Monastery of Vlatadon), Berea, Athens (100+images), Corinth (50+ images), Cenchrea, Ephesus Site, Ephesus Artifacts, Caesarea (100+ images), Antioch On the Orontes.  (completed 9 October 2013).

Paul's Third Missionary Journey (Acts 18:23–21:14)

Antioch on the Orontes, Seleucia Pieria,  Ephesus Site, Ephesus Artifacts, South Philippi, North Philippi), Troas, Assos Citadel, Temple, Harbor, Assos Agora, Necropolis, Theater, Miletus, Patara, Patara Lighthouse, Caesarea (100+ images).  There are other places, such as Corinth, Thessalonica, Berea, etc., that Paul probably visited on this journey.  See a map in a good Atlas to get an idea of the route of this Third Journey. (completed 17 October 2013).

The Seven Churchs of Revelation  (Revelation 1–3)

Ephesus Site, Ephesus Artifacts, Ephesus Paul/Thecla Cave; Smyrna, Smyrna Artifacts; Pergamum Acropolis, Pergamum Asclepion, Pergamum Artifacts, Pergamum Red Basilica; Thyatira; Philadelphia; Sardis; Laodicea.

Some Recommended Readings  (back to top)

I am pleased to announce that Zondervan has released a 13 Lesson Video series that we filmed in the Holy Land.  This is an 18-minute Introduction to the ancient Middle East.  A Trailer for the series can be found Here.  This video is part of their "MasterLectures" and is available streaming there and also as a DVD set on amazon.com.  For an announcement on Todd Bolen's blog, Click Here.

Two samples of this 13 Lesson Series are available on YouTube.

  1. Here is an 18-minute Introduction to the Middle East.
  2. Here is a 6-minute video Introduction to Jerusalem.

Zondervan Atlas of the Bible (Carl Rasmussen) is not only helpful for Geographical studies, but also for Old Testament and New Testament classes on the History of Israel contains relevant text, maps, and pictures.  The "Geographical" section in the Atlas is especially helpful, compare to other atlases.

For very accurate maps of Israel and Jordan see the 7 map set (Regional Study Maps) available from BiblicalBackgrounds (Jim Monson and Steve Lancaster) along with all their interactive materials.

Travel  (back to top)

Dr. Carl Rasmussen is leading an 18-day "Adult Study Tour" called "In the Footsteps of Paul: Turkey and Greece" (May 23–June 19, 2021).  Contact Dr. Rasmussen for details.

Dr. Carl Rsmussen is leading a 12-Day Tour called "Following Paul from Shipwreck on Malta to Martyrdom in Rome" (May 11–22, 2021).  Contact Dr. Rasmussen for details.

For your convenience we are introcuding a section called "Topical Easy Find."  We think you will find it useful for your personal study as well as when you are preparing a lecture or sermon in a given topic.  Our most recent additon is The First Missionary Journey of Paul where places associated with the journey are listed.  The following if from that section:

Paul's First Missionary Journey (Acts 13:1–14:28)  Antioch on the Orontes, Seleucia Pieria, Paphos, Perga (city, stadium, theater, bath), Pisidian Antioch (artifacts), Iconium, Lystra, Derbe, Attalia.  (Completed 3 September 2013)

In Topical Easy Find  have assembled my suggestions for images related to a variety of topics.  For example, if you are studying the life of Solomon I have assembled images that are related to that topic that you might want to check.  As always, you are welcome to use our images in your personal PowerPoint presentations—if you could give credit (and/or a link) to www.HolyLandPhotos.org that would be appreciated.

page_Title: 15 Day Bible Study Tour to Turkey April/May 2024
page_Text:
Led by Dr. Carl Rasmussen – April 25–May 9, 2024.  You are invited to join this "once in a lifetime" trip.  Study the New Testament in one of the lands of the New Testament!  — Including the Seven Churches of Revelation!

Email Dr. Rasmussen for an Itinerary and Details

2foot.steps.tours+TKHL@gmail.com

Greetings!   Mary and I invite you to join us for a 15-day  "Bible Study Tour" to Turkey. We have a handcrafted itinerary and an excellent guide.

After visiting Istanbul, we will fly to Antioch on the Orontes (Antakya), the third-largest city in Paul's day,  where believers were first called "Christians" and where Paul began all three of his missionary journies.  We will visit Seleucia, the port where the first journey set sail (Acts 13:4), Tarsus (the home of Paul), Derbe, Lystra, Iconium, and Pisidian Antioch—places not usually visited by tours but all of which are of biblical importance.  After stopping for two nights in the Mediterranian city of Antalya we will travel to Colossae, and then begin our exploration of the Seven Churches of Revelation—with many stops along the way (see itinerary).

 

 

 

I will be giving mini-lectures along the way both on the bus and on the sites, drawing from my studies and from the 25+ trips that we have led to Turkey and Greece.  We will relate what we are seeing to the New Testament and the Early Christian Church.  Thus, this is not a mere tour, but a hands-on experience as we study the Bible and its Greco-Roman background together!

You will be amazed at what you will be learning along the way and April and early May are perfect—not too hot, not too cool, and the wildflowers are still in bloom in some parts of the country!

Tour Dates and Cost:  April 18–May 2, 2024.  Cost: $5,190 per person in Double Occupancy; add $1,390 Single Room supplement; Includes airfare from major US cities to Istanbul and return from Izmir. 

We hope you will join us!  Contact us soon if you are interested (2foot.steps.tours+TKHP@gmail.com).

Carl and Mary Rasmussen

Above: Exploring Perga (Acts 13:13-14).

PS This trip is followed by a Bible Study Tour to Greece and you are welcome to join that as well!

I am Professor Emeritus of Old Testament at Bethel University in St. Paul, Minnesota.  I received my Ph.D. in Ancient Near Eastern Studies from Dropsie University.  Currently, I am an adjunct professor at the Jerusalem University College in Jerusalem and Bethel University in St. Paul, MN, and have served as a consultant for a publisher and several media organizations.

Zondervan has published an updated version of my Zondervan Atlas of the Bible.  Lavishly illustrated, the new version has been updated in every way including new maps, pictures, graphics, and updated text.  Zondervan has also published my Zondervan Essential Atlas of the Bible.  In addition, I maintain the website www.HolylandPhotos.org which features over 7.000 free, high-quality, and high-resolution images of Israel, Turkey, Greece, Jordan, Italy, and Malta.

page_Title: 13–Day Tour — Exploring Biblical Israel from Dan to Beersheba Oct 2024
page_Text:
Greetings!  From October 15 to 27, 2024 Mary and I will be leading a 13-day trip to Israel during which time we will Explore Biblical Israel from Dan in the north to Beersheba in the south.

This trip not only includes Jerusalem, sites associated with the ministry of Jesus around the Sea of Galilee, and Hazor, Dan, and Caesarea Philippi in the north, but also some not-so-frequently visited places such as Arad, Beersheba, Lachish (second most important Judean city after Jerusalem), Azekah (David and Goliath), Beth Shemesh (Samson), Shiloh (Tabernacle and Ark of Covenant), Mount Gerizim (Samaritan Temple), and Samaria (capital city of Ahab and Jezebel and others).

I will be giving mini-lectures along the way both on the bus and on the sites, drawing from my studies. I have spent 16 years of my adult life living in, and guiding, academic groups in Israel, Jordan, Greece, and Turkey (including living, teaching, and guiding in Jerusalem for 7 years).

Tour Dates and Cost:  October 15–27, 2024.  Cost: $4,990 per person in Double Occupancy; add $1,365 Single Room supplement; Includes airfare from New York to TLV and return. 

For an Itinerary and additional information contact us soon (2foot.steps.tours+HLPExplorI@gmail.com).

You are invited to join us!

Carl and Mary Rasmussen

page_Title: Following Paul from Shipwreck on Malta to Martyrdom in Rome — a 14–Day Tour
page_Text:
Mary and I are pleased to announce for the SECOND TIME our popular trip (14-day tour) entitled Following Paul from Shipwreck on Malta to Rome (Acts 27–28)!  April 27–May 10, 2022.

Email Dr. Rasmussen for an Itinerary and Details

2footstepstours@gmail.com

Greetings! Mary and I are excited to invite you to join our handcrafted adult “study” trip entitled Following Paul from Shipwreck on Malta to Martyrdom in Rome. We begin our tour on Malta where we will explore the bays where the shipwreck of Paul may have occurred as well as the Island of Malta. Mark Gatt, who discovered an anchor that may have been jettisoned from the ship that Paul was on, will guide us on land and on the water! Then, just as Paul left Malta, we will ferry to Sicily where we will spend a day and a half exploring the antiquities. From there, we will travel north to Pompeii where we will visit the well–preserved remains of the Roman town that was destroyed by the eruption of the volcano Vesuvius in AD 79. It has a lot to teach us about life in New Testament times!

After exploring Puteoli, where Paul landed (Acts 28:13), we will head to Rome—following in the footsteps of Paul along the via Appia.

We will spend four and a half days exploring the wonderful archaeological and traditional sites of Rome—the place Paul was imprisoned twice, and eventually martyred (as was Peter).  Dr. Thompson and I will be giving mini-lectures along the way both on the bus and on the sites. We will relate what we are seeing to the New Testament and the Early Christian Church. Thus, this is not a mere tour, but a hands-on experience as we study the New Testament and its Greco Roman background together! We invite you to join us on this unique, exciting, adventure! Contact me soon for additional details.

We hope you will join us!  Contact us soon if you are interested (2footstepstours@gmail.com).

Carl and Mary Rasmussen

The image above is of the Luzzu that took us out to the place where Paul's ship anchored before being wrecked on a sandbar.   After spending 3 months on Malta, after the shipwreck, Paul set sail to Rome from there.

page_Title: 9 Day Bible Study Tour of Greece — May 2024
page_Text:
Greetings!    In May 8–16, 2024 Mary and I will be leading a 9-day Bible Study Tour of Greece.

Our Bible Study Tour will begin in Philippi, where the "Good News" was first preached in Europe.  We will visit biblical places such as Thessalonica, Berea, Athens, Corinth, and Cenchrea—as well as World Heritage sites such as a monastery in Meteora and Delphi.  We will stop at the beautiful museum that contains lavish gold artifacts from the tomb of Philip II, the father of Alexander the Great.

I will be giving mini-lectures along the way both on the bus and on the sites, drawing from my studies. I have spent 16 years of my adult life living in, and guiding, academic groups in Israel, Jordan, Greece, and Turkey (including living, teaching, and guiding in Jerusalem for 7 years).

Tour Dates and Cost: May 8–16, 2024.  Cost: $3,990 per person in Double Occupancy; add $690 Single Room supplement; Includes roundtrip airfare from major US cities to Greece and return. 

For an Itinerary and additional information contact us soon (2foot.steps.tours+GHL@gmail.com).

You are invited to join us!

Carl and Mary Rasmussen

page_Title: Bible Study Tour to Israel/Jordan, Turkey, and Greece — April-May, 2024
page_Text:
I am pleased to announce, in conjunction with Tutku Tours, that we will be offering a comprehensive 35-day Bible Study Tour to Israel/Jordan, Turkey, and Greece. You are invited to join this "once in a lifetime" trip.  Study the Bible in the lands of the Bible!  From April 5 through May 9, 2024.  To view a map of the trip, Click Here.

Email Dr. Rasmussen for an Itinerary and Details.

In the Israel/Jordan segment of the trip (April 5–18, 2024 ) we will begin in Jerusalem with a study of the city of Jerusalem.  As we head north along the Sharon Plain we will visit Caesarea-on-the-sea and Mount Carmel and Megiddo.  We will be staying on the shore of the Sea of Galilee and visiting sites associated with the ministry of Jesus.  In addition, we will travel north to visit biblical places such as Hazor, Dan, and Caesarea Philippi.  After this, we will cross into Jordan and visit Jerash, Mt. Nebo, and Petra.   Crossing back into Israel by Elat on the Red Sea (swim) we will travel north to the Dead Sea, visiting Tamar Biblical Park on the way.  The next day we will visit Masada, Arad, and Mamshit before returning to our hotel for the night.  Our final day will include floating on the Dead Sea and visits to Ein Gedi, Qumran, and (time permitting) the Jordan River and Jericho. Email me for the complete itinerary.

In the Turkey segment of the trip (April 19–May 2, 2024), we will begin in Istanbul visiting the Hagia Sophia and the world-class Archaeology Museum.   From Istanbul, we will fly to Antioch-on-the-Orontes River, the third-largest city in Paul's day, where believers were first called "Christians" and where Paul began all three of his missionary journeys.  We will visit Seleucia, the port where the first journey set sail (Acts 13:4), Tarsus (the home of Paul), Derbe, Lystra, Iconium, and Pisidian Antioch—places not usually visited by tours but all of which are of biblical importance. After stopping for two nights in the Mediterranian city of Antalya we will travel to Colossae, and then begin our exploration of all of the Seven Churches of Revelation—with many stops  (email me for the complete itinerary).

In the Greece segment of the trip (May 3–9, 2024), we will begin in Thessaloniki in northern Greece.  We will begin touring with a day trip to Philippi, where the "Good News" was first preached in Europe.  As we motor towards Athens we will be stopping along the way at Berea, Vergina, Meteora, and Delphi—with overnights near Meteora and Delphi.  In Athens, we will visit the Acropolis, the Areopagus ("Mars Hill"), and the new Acropolis Museum.  We will take a day trip to Corinth and Cenchrea. Email Dr. Rasmussen for an Itinerary and Details.

If you wish, you may join one or more "segments" of this trip.

In preparation for Christmas the following links will take you to Nazareth where the annunciation took place; to Bethlehem where Jesus was born; to an example of a manger (also, use Search Window); and to the Herodium where Herod the Great had a pleasure palace and where he was buried.

To the left, the Grotto of the Nativity in the Church of the Nativity in Bethlehem.

To visit the original home of Saint Nicholas at Myra in Turkey(!), click here!

page_Title: Oct 2024 — Exploring Biblcal Anatolia and Eastern Turkey
page_Text:
Greetings!  From October 4 to 14, 2024 Mary and I will be leading an 11-day trip to Türkiye during which time we will Explore Biblical Anatolia and Southeastern Türkiye, from Ankara to Haran.

We invite you to join us as we Explore Biblical Anatolia and Southeastern Turkey—from the Ankara area (the homeland of the Hittites) to Haran (the
homeland of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob). Along the way, we will visit the UNESCO “World Heritage Sites” of Hattusha (capital of the Hittites), Goreme National Park (Cappadocia where early Christianity flourished), and Gobekli Tepe (unique neolithic remains).  Included will be stops at Tarsus (home of Saul/Paul), Antioch (Acts 11), and Seleucia Piera (Saul, Barnabas, and John Mark set sail from here to begin the First Journey). The museums that we will visit exhibit some of the most outstanding mosaics from the Roman world. Along the way, I will be giving mini-lectures to compliment the commentary of our attentive, expert guide. We will enjoy excellent accommodations and meals. We will be visiting some of our favorite sites in Turkey that we have visited many times before, on private or university trips.  Please email me to receive a descriptive brochure!  I can be contacted at 2FootStepsTours+HLPExETurk@gmail.com

I will be giving mini-lectures along the way both on the bus and on the sites, drawing from my studies and from the 25+ trips that we have led to Turkey and the Eastern Mediterranean.  We will relate what we are seeing to the Old and New Testaments and the Early Christian Church.  Thus, this is not a mere tour, but a hands-on experience as we study the Bible and its cultural and geographical setting!

Tour Dates and Cost:  October 4-14, 2024.  Cost: $3,990 per person in Double Occupancy; add $460 Single Room supplement; Includes airfare from New York to Istanbul and return from Istanbul to New York—and the internal flights from Istanbul to Ankara and from Urfa to Istanbul.  We are now accepting Registrations and Deposits for this trip.  If you wish to reserve a space, contact me.

In the Footsteps of The Apostles: Turkey AND Greece

You are invited to join our 18-day tour to Turkey and Greece that Mary and I are leading in response to those who have asked us to put together a “not for credit” study tour—April 20 – May 7, 2026.  I will be giving mini-lectures along the way both on the bus and on the sites, drawing from my studies and from the 30 trips that we have led to Turkey and Greece.  We will relate what we are seeing to the New Testament and the Early Christian Church.  Thus, it is not a mere tour, but a hands-on experience as we study the New Testament and its Greco-Roman background together!

Noteworthy!   We will visit all 7 churches mentioned in Revelation 1-3 and places where 15 of the 27 New Testament books were written to and/or from!  You will be amazed at what you will be learning along the way and April/May is perfect—not too hot and not too cold!

To receive additional information contact Dr. Rasmussen at go2CarlRasm+HLP@gmail.com.
